When build fails in 02-build due to redefinition of 'Ca6k00045Custom' in op_proto.h caused by manual OP_ADD in host_tiling_src while autogen REG_OP already defines the operator, do not add a second registration with OP_ADD; instead remove the manual OP_ADD and rely solely on the autogen REG_OP.

When opbuild fails with redefinition of `class ge::op::Ca6k00322Custom` in `autogen/op_proto.h`, do not add a duplicate `REG_OP` block in op host source files; instead remove any extra `REG_OP` definition and rely on the single definition in the auto-generated header.

repair_memory_selection.selection_rationale: All five entries address build failures caused by redefinition of the operator class in autogen/op_proto.h due to duplicate REG_OP macros or conflicting OpDef additions. The current failure for ca6k_00390 shows the identical 'redefinition of class ge::op::Ca6k00390Custom' error. id=3fea4f99 is directly for ca6k_00390 and advises against ignoring duplicate names. The other four (from ca6k_00045, ca6k_00322, ca6k_00285, ca6k_00147) each describe the same class of redefinition error and prescribe removing duplicate REG_OP or avoiding manual OP_ADD when autogen already provides one. These provide complementary examples of how to eliminate the duplicate, covering both REG_OP-only and manual OP_ADD cases.
